sourcePage.rawText:
4.     The complete set of values of for which       1      2    0 is




       A            1, 1            2

       B            1,          2

       C       1           2

       D           1,       2

       E       1           1,       2




© UCLES 2017

stem.rawText: The complete set of values of for which       1      2    0 is

solution.rawText:
We can factorise the given inequality to give (x + 1)(x − 1)(x − 2) > 0. The function f(x) =
(x + 1)(x − 1)(x − 2) is zero when x = −1, x = 1 and x = 2.
We can now make a table showing the signs of the three factors for different values of x. (This
is a useful technique in general.)

                 x < −1      x = −1   −1 < x < 1   x=1     1<x<2       x=2     x>2
         x+1       −            0         +         +        +          +       +
         x−1       −           −          −         0        +          +       +
         x−2       −           −          −         −        −          0       +
         f(x)      −            0         +         0        −          0       +

Therefore f(x) is positive when −1 < x < 1 and when x > 2, so the answer is E.
